scope: Production-shaped planning review proxy for selecting the explicit advisor model

protocol.cost_scope: Counterfactual route cost including the shared draft and any review and reconciliation requests
protocol.privacy: Source, prompts, rubrics, raw answers, grader keys, paths, symbols, session identifiers, and repository identity are excluded

decision.status: historical aggregate; no universal advisor selected
decision.reason: Opus had the highest mean of cluster medians and the lowest total observed cost among reviewed advisor routes. It cost 18.79% more than leaving the draft untreated while improving the mean cluster median by 6.5 points.
decision.policy: Keep automatic advisor review off. Independent review may be developer-invoked, but this small proxy does not justify a universal advisor model.

limitations: The model ranking is based on two workload clusters and one shared draft per cluster; three graders reduce grading noise but do not increase workload sample size.
The strongest reviewer changed by workload: Sonnet led the first cluster and Opus led the second.

The benchmark passed a bounded transcript to the reviewer and asked Terra to reconcile the advice. A developer-invoked isolated review is a different treatment, so the model ranking does not transfer directly.
The study measures plan revision, not implementation correctness, shipping outcomes, or the causal value of invoking independent review on a production task.
Raw prompts, outputs, and per-judge artifacts are not attached to this public aggregate.
